_readme: Full XED exports for Registry-style use. For a minimal webhook instance (_demoemea.content, offerMetadata, thresholds only) see samples/ajo-webhook-payload-with-webhookName.json.
webhookName: Replace with journey / custom action label

destinationURL.title: destinationURL
destinationURL.description: certain assets like images can have a destination, when clicked upon. eg) http://ecommerce.com/product

sourceURL.title: sourceURL
sourceURL.description: source URL of the asset. eg) URL of an image asset http://cdn.com/myimage.png

trackedURL.title: trackedURL
trackedURL.description: tracked URL of destination URL. This field would be set by the decisioning system to track click on destination URL.

xdmSchemaDefinitions.title: Personalized Offer Items - Experience Decisioning
xdmSchemaDefinitions.description: Autogenerated schema for Personalized Offer Items - Experience Decisioning

_id.title: Identifier
_id.description: A unique identifier for the record.

itemCalendarConstraints.title: Item Calendar Constraints
itemCalendarConstraints.description: Defines the date range during which this decision item is eligible. This constraint may not be strictly enforced always.

endDate.title: End Date & Time
endDate.description: The end date of a decision options validity. Options that have passed their end date can no longer cannot be proposed in the decisioning process.

startDate.title: Start Date & Time
startDate.description: The start date of a decision options validity.. Options that have not reached their start date cannot be proposed yet in the decisioning process.

itemCatalogID.title: Item CatalogId
itemCatalogID.description: Each decision item belong to a catalog. CatalogId is used to relate to the catalog. Collections are derived based on select queries and catalogId helps to avoid joins with DecisionCatalog table during query execution in hub and especially the edges.

itemConstraints.title: Item Constraints
itemConstraints.description: Constraints define the policies that ensure a particular decision Item qualifies, is relevant and is suitable for a particular profile. Such policy rules determine the eligibility, applicability and suitability of the option. Usually the option is tested against a user profile and the events that were observed for that profile, but the qualification rule can consider any context data as long as it can be described by an XDM schema.

description.title: Description
description.description: Profile constraint description. It is used to convey human readable intentions on how or why this profile constraint was constructed and/or what option will be included or excluded by it.

eligibilityRule.title: Eligibility Rule
eligibilityRule.description: A reference to a decision rule that evaluates to true or false for a given profile and/or other given contextual XDM objects. The rule is used to decide if the option qualifies for a given profile. The value is the URI (@id) of the decision rule that is referenced. See schema https://ns.adobe.com/experience/decisioning/rule

profileConstraintType.title: Profile Constraint Type

meta:enum.eligibilityRule: The profile constraint is expressed as a single rule that must evaluate to true before the constrained action is allowed
meta:enum.anySegments: The profile constraint is expressed as one or more segments and the profile must be a member of at least one of them before the constrained action is allowed
meta:enum.allSegments: The profile constraint is expressed as one or more segments and the profile must be a member of all of them before the constrained action is allowed
meta:enum.rules: The profile constraint is expressed as a number of different rules, e.g. eligibility, applicability, suitability, which all must evaluate to true before the constrained action is allowed

profileConstraintType.description: Determines if any constraints are currently set and how the contraints are expressed. It could be though a rule or through one or more segment memberships.

_id.title: Identifier
_id.description: Identity of the segment in the related namespace.

namespace.title: Namespace
namespace.description: The namespace associated with the `xid` attribute.

code.title: Code
code.description: The code is a human readable identifier for the namespace and can be used to request the technical namespace id which is used for identity graph processing.

xid.title: Experience identifier
xid.description: When present, this value represents a cross-namespace identifier that is unique across all namespace-scoped identifiers in all namespaces.

itemPriority.title: Item Priority
itemPriority.description: Priority of a decision item, based on which decision items could be ranked

itemTags.title: Item Tags
itemTags.description: An array of Unified TagIds associated with the decision item.

trackingToken.title: Tracking Token
trackingToken.description: A placeholder attribute to be added to the message to indicate the item interaction will be tracked. The value is meant to be populated by the AJO and customer should not populate with any value.

contentReferences.title: Content References
contentReferences.description: Content References to be associated with offer

additionalProperties.title: Reference Details
additionalProperties.description: Content Reference Details supported through AJO or External Fragments

publishDate.title: Publish date
publishDate.description: Publish date & time of the fragment at AJO (outside of ExD)

contentReferencesMap.title: Content References
contentReferencesMap.description: Content References to be associated with offer

additionalProperties.title: Reference Details
additionalProperties.description: Content Reference Details supported through AJO or External Fragments

publishDate.title: Publish date
publishDate.description: Publish date & time of the fragment at AJO (outside of ExD)

etag.title: ETag
etag.description: Entity tag for versioning of the offer item. Used for optimistic concurrency control.

fCapConstraintsLastIndex.title: Frequency Capping Constraints Last Index
fCapConstraintsLastIndex.description: Last index of the capping object. This field is hidden on UI.

items.title: Frequency capping constraint
items.description: Frequency capping constraint defines additional rules based on prior offer proposition, either across all audiences or for a particular profile identity.

customQuery.title: Custom PQL query
customQuery.description: Custom PQL query to match events.

dynamicCappingThresholdEnabled.title: Toggle for Static or Dynamic Capping Threshold
dynamicCappingThresholdEnabled.description: If set to true, PQL query will be used to determine the capping threshold. The UI will not show limit field when this is enabled and will show a text box to enter PQL query.

dynamicCappingThresholdQuery.title: PQL Query for Dynamic Capping Threshold
dynamicCappingThresholdQuery.description: PQL query to determine the capping threshold.

events.title: Events
events.description: Events of the capping to comply with. Since most of the events don't support offerId level counting, we will start with only support decisioning event, and will extend to support custom event in future phases. Other events to be supported in the future : web-display, web-click, message-delivery, message-open, message-click.

index.title: Index
index.description: Index of the capping object, this is used as suffix for counter id when we have multi capping objects.  Index starts from 1

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: How many times an option can be proposed.

repeat.title: Repeat
repeat.description: When true, new counter rule with updated time window will be created. Configure a capping to be repeated (e.g., max times every 1 week). ECS will support repeating config in the rules, scan rules daily, and create a new rule with new expiry when the rule is due for repeating.

enabled.title: Enabled
enabled.description: If set to true ECS will created new counter rules with updated time window.

unit.title: Unit
unit.description: Unit to comply with.  It will be day/week/month.

meta:enum.global: Global cap is a constraint on how many times an option can be proposed in totality.
meta:enum.profile: Profile cap is a constraint on how many times an option can be proposed to a certain profile.

lifecycleStatus.title: life cycle status
lifecycleStatus.description: Status associated with offer life cycle.

etag.title: ETag
etag.description: Entity tag for versioning of the offer item. Used for optimistic concurrency control.
